Defines the `TickType_t` type as a 32-bit unsigned integer.

# Purpose
This is a simple C header file that defines a type alias for a 32-bit unsigned integer. The `typedef` statement creates a new type name, `TickType_t`, which is an alias for `uint32_t`. This alias can be used throughout the codebase to represent a tick count or time-related value, providing clarity and abstraction over the underlying data type. The `#pragma once` directive ensures that the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
